tool.name: SOPA Resolve
tool.description: Resolve patch conflicts in segmented data

param.name: method_name
param.label: Segmentation method
param.option: Cellpose - This can be done on all patches directly, or on one individual patch.
Stardist - This can be done on all patches directly, or on one individual patch.
Comseg - This can be done on all patches directly, or on one individual patch.
Baysor - This can be done on all patches directly, or on one individual patch.

expand.label: Comma separated names of the directories containing the cellpose segmentation on patches (or multiple directories if using multi-step segmentation)
Comma separated names of the directories containing the stardist segmentation on patches (or multiple directories if using multi-step segmentation)

tool.help:
**What it does**
SOPA Resolve merges overlapping cell boundaries from per-patch segmentation into a single, coherent segmentation for the whole slide. When segmentation methods (Cellpose, Stardist, Baysor, or Comseg) are run on individual patches, cells near patch boundaries may be duplicated or split across patches. This tool resolves those conflicts to produce a unified segmentation.
**When to use this tool**
Use this tool **after** running SOPA Segmentation on patched data. It is only needed for segmentation methods that support patch-based processing:
- **Cellpose** — resolves overlapping cell boundaries from image patches - **Stardist** — resolves overlapping cell boundaries from image patches - **Baysor** — resolves overlapping cell boundaries from transcript patches; supports a ``--min-area`` filter - **Comseg** — resolves overlapping cell boundaries from transcript patches; supports a ``--min-area`` filter
**When NOT to use this tool**
Proseg and Tissue segmentation do not use patches, so the resolve step is not needed for those methods (the SOPA Segmentation tool already runs them on the whole slide directly).
**Parameters**
- **Min area** (Baysor and Comseg only): Cells with an area below this threshold (in pixels²) will be removed during the resolve step. This is useful for filtering out small segmentation artifacts at patch boundaries.
**Workflow**
1. Run **SOPA Patchify** to divide the slide into patches 2. Run **SOPA Segmentation** on each patch (Cellpose, Stardist, Baysor, or Comseg) 3. Run **SOPA Resolve** to merge the per-patch segmentations into one coherent result
**More Information**
See the `SOPA resolve documentation <https://prism-oncology.github.io/sopa/tutorials/cli_usage/#resolve-patch-overlaps>`_.
